name: Reward Tier Selector
description: Campaign reward picker for founder packs, preorder bundles, beta access tiers, and collector editions rendered as physical cartridges or boxed launch packs rather than generic pricing cards.

cartridge-row.description: Horizontal reward cartridges with price, contents, inventory meter, delivery window, and select action.

slots.tier-cartridge: Repeating reward object shaped like a product cartridge or boxed set, not a generic card.
slots.tier-title: Founder Pack, Early Access, Collector Kit, Studio Bundle, or similar.
slots.price: Large price or pledge amount with billing/preorder note.
slots.contents: Short inventory list of included items or access rights.
slots.availability: Limited quantity, sold out, shipping window, or beta wave state.
slots.select-action: Choose reward, reserve, or join waitlist button.

comparison-console.description: Wide comparison console with tiers as columns, feature rows as manual-style line items, and a sticky action row.

slots.tier-header-row: Tier names, prices, and featured labels.
slots.feature-row: Manual-like included feature row with icon/check state.

visual_brief: A reward section that looks like choosing product cartridges or boxed founder kits. Each tier should have a strong silhouette, hard borders, a top notch or label strip, visible price, inventory/status strip, and delivery timing. Avoid soft SaaS pricing cards, pill badges, three-column gray boxes, and generic feature checklists. The selected tier should feel mechanically active, like it snapped into a console slot or got pressed down into the deck.

responsive.mobile: Use a vertical selector with one tier per full-width cartridge. Keep price and select action visible without horizontal scrolling.
responsive.desktop: Three to four tiers in a row or a wide comparison console. Featured tier can be slightly taller but must not become a nested card.

layout_hints.tier_language: Use campaign words: reward, founder pack, beta key, collector kit, early-bird, preorder bundle.
layout_hints.availability: Show scarcity honestly with inventory or delivery labels, not vague urgency.
layout_hints.selection_state: Selected tier should use border/fill/press state, not only color.

micro.select: cartridge shifts down 3px and shows active border over 120ms
micro.inventory: meter fills once when tier enters viewport

transitions.tier-stagger: Tiers snap in from left to right with short stagger.

keyboard: Arrow keys move between reward tiers.
Enter or Space selects the focused tier.
Tab moves into the selected tier action.
accessibility.focus_management: Selected and focused states must be visually distinct for keyboard users.
